content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary array arrarrarr whose elements are only 000 and 111. Your task is to find the length of the longest subarray that contains only 1s1s1s after flipping exactly one contiguous subarray from 000 to 111.




You must perform the flip operation at least once.




Input Format

The first line contains one integer nnn, the size of the array.                                                                                                               - Next line contains nnn integers arr[0],arr[1]...arr[n]arr[0],arr[1]...arr[n]arr[0],arr[1]...arr[n], representing the elements of the array.


Output Format

Find length of longest subarray with 111 after flipping one contiguous subarray.
constraints: Sum of n over all test cases does not exceed 100000
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 100000) – the size of the array. The second line contains n integers separated by spaces, each being 0 or 1.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum possible length of a subarray consisting only of 1s after flipping exactly one contiguous subarray of 0s to 1s (the flipped subarray may be empty).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Flipping the single zero at either end yields an array of all 1s, so the longest all‑1 subarray has length 3.
Flipping the whole array (the only contiguous block of zeros) turns it into 1 1 1, giving length 3.
The array already consists of only 1s; the longest subarray of 1s is the whole array.

solution.approach: The problem reduces to finding the longest subarray that contains at most one zero, because we can flip that zero (or an empty block) to obtain all 1s. Using a sliding‑window (two‑pointer) technique we maintain a window [left,right] with zeroCount≤1. When extending right, if zeroCount becomes 2 we move left forward until the window is valid again. The window size right‑left+1 is a candidate answer; the maximum over the scan is the required length. This runs in O(n) time per test case with O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, O(total n) over all test cases
